Tests that the absolute value of negative infinity is positive infinity in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that verifies the behavior of the `Math.abs` function when applied to `Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY`. The test checks that the absolute value of negative infinity is equal to positive infinity, which is the expected behavior according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. This code provides narrow functionality as it focuses on a specific mathematical operation and its expected result. It is likely used in a testing context to ensure that the JavaScript environment adheres to the correct mathematical standards.
